title: rubus hirtus var. bellardii
intro: Rubus hirtus var. bellardii, also known as the bellard's thimbleberry, is a variant of the thimbleberry plant, a type of wild fruit-bearing shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. bellardii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and can be consumed fresh, dried, or made into jams and preserves.
